slides.title: PhD CET Mock Exam 2 - Computer Science
slides.subtitle: 50 Practice MCQs for Doctoral Entrance Examination • Topic 1-5

slides.question: Q1: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is the process of simplifying a Boolean expression to reduce the number of logic gates required for implementation.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Boolean Algebra Minimization** is defined as: is the process of simplifying a Boolean expression to reduce the number of logic gates required for implementation. (logic gate reduction optimization).

slides.question: Q2: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a graphical method used to simplify Boolean algebra expressions without using algebraic laws.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Karnaugh Map (K-map)** is defined as: is a graphical method used to simplify Boolean algebra expressions without using algebraic laws. (graphical boolean simplification table).

slides.question: Q3: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a combinational logic circuit that selects binary information from one of many input lines and directs it to a single output.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Multiplexer (MUX)** is defined as: is a combinational logic circuit that selects binary information from one of many input lines and directs it to a single output. (many-to-one signal selector).

slides.question: Q4: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a combinational logic circuit that takes a single input line and routes it to one of several digital outputs.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Demultiplexer (DEMUX)** is defined as: is a combinational logic circuit that takes a single input line and routes it to one of several digital outputs. (one-to-many signal router).

slides.question: Q5: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a combinational circuit that adds three single-bit inputs (A, B, and Carry-in) and outputs Sum and Carry.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Full Adder** is defined as: is a combinational circuit that adds three single-bit inputs (A, B, and Carry-in) and outputs Sum and Carry. (three-input binary addition circuit).

slides.question: Q6: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'are sequential logic circuits used as basic memory storage elements, changing states based on clock inputs (e.g. JK, D).'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Flip-Flops** is defined as: are sequential logic circuits used as basic memory storage elements, changing states based on clock inputs (e.g. JK, D). (clock-driven sequential memory components).

slides.question: Q7: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a digital counter where all flip-flops are triggered simultaneously by a single common clock pulse.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Synchronous Counter** is defined as: is a digital counter where all flip-flops are triggered simultaneously by a single common clock pulse. (simultaneously-clocked state counter).

slides.question: Q8: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a combinational circuit that converts an n-bit binary input code into a maximum of 2^n unique output lines.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Decoder** is defined as: is a combinational circuit that converts an n-bit binary input code into a maximum of 2^n unique output lines. (binary-to-unary line converter).

slides.question: Q9: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a computer design model featuring a single shared memory and bus for both instruction and data storage.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Von Neumann Architecture** is defined as: is a computer design model featuring a single shared memory and bus for both instruction and data storage. (shared instruction-data bus architecture).

slides.question: Q10: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a multi-level layout (L1, L2, L3) of small, fast memory to bridge the speed gap between CPU and RAM.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Cache Memory Hierarchy** is defined as: is a multi-level layout (L1, L2, L3) of small, fast memory to bridge the speed gap between CPU and RAM. (multi-level fast memory staging).

slides.question: Q11: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is the smallest unit of execution within a process, sharing the process's memory space but maintaining its own stack and registers.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Thread** is defined as: is the smallest unit of execution within a process, sharing the process's memory space but maintaining its own stack and registers. (lightweight shared-memory execution unit).

slides.question: Q12: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a mutual exclusion locking mechanism used to synchronize access to a resource, ensuring only one thread holds the lock at a time.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Mutex** is defined as: is a mutual exclusion locking mechanism used to synchronize access to a resource, ensuring only one thread holds the lock at a time. (single-thread resource lock).

slides.question: Q13: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a synchronization tool that uses a signaling integer value to control access to shared resources by multiple processes.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Counting Semaphore** is defined as: is a synchronization tool that uses a signaling integer value to control access to shared resources by multiple processes. (variable-based process signaling).

slides.question: Q14: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is the process of storing the state of a CPU process/thread so that it can be restored and resume execution later.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Context Switching** is defined as: is the process of storing the state of a CPU process/thread so that it can be restored and resume execution later. (cpu state saving-restoring overhead).

slides.question: Q15: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'refers to mechanisms (like pipes, message queues, shared memory) that allow processes to communicate and share data.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Inter-Process Communication (IPC)** is defined as: refers to mechanisms (like pipes, message queues, shared memory) that allow processes to communicate and share data. (isolated process communication mechanisms).

slides.question: Q16: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'uses temporary memory areas to store data during transfers between fast devices (like CPU) and slow I/O peripherals.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **I/O Buffering** is defined as: uses temporary memory areas to store data during transfers between fast devices (like CPU) and slow I/O peripherals. (speed-matching data storage buffer).

slides.question: Q17: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is the process of managing I/O requests for a hard disk drive using algorithms like SCAN, C-SCAN, or SSTF.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Disk Scheduling** is defined as: is the process of managing I/O requests for a hard disk drive using algorithms like SCAN, C-SCAN, or SSTF. (hard drive head seek optimization).

slides.question: Q18: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'include Contiguous, Linked, and Indexed allocation strategies for storing files on physical storage devices.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **File Allocation Methods** is defined as: include Contiguous, Linked, and Indexed allocation strategies for storing files on physical storage devices. (physical disk block mapping strategies).

slides.question: Q19: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'provide the essential interface between a running program and the operating system kernel to request privileged services.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **System Calls** is defined as: provide the essential interface between a running program and the operating system kernel to request privileged services. (privileged kernel service requests interface).

slides.question: Q20: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'designate different configurations of multiple physical disks to achieve redundancy, performance, or both (e.g. RAID 0, 1, 5).'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **RAID Levels** is defined as: designate different configurations of multiple physical disks to achieve redundancy, performance, or both (e.g. RAID 0, 1, 5). (redundancy-performance disk array configurations).

slides.question: Q21: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'describes the asymptotic upper bound of an algorithm's execution time or space requirement in terms of input size.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Big O Complexity** is defined as: describes the asymptotic upper bound of an algorithm's execution time or space requirement in terms of input size. (asymptotic run-time upper bound indicator).

slides.question: Q22: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a linear data structure that follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle for inserting and removing elements.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Stack Data Structure** is defined as: is a linear data structure that follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle for inserting and removing elements. (LIFO element storage collection).

slides.question: Q23: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a linear data structure that follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle for inserting and removing elements.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Queue Data Structure** is defined as: is a linear data structure that follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle for inserting and removing elements. (FIFO element storage collection).

slides.question: Q24: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a linear collection of data nodes where each node points to the next node in the sequence.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Singly Linked List** is defined as: is a linear collection of data nodes where each node points to the next node in the sequence. (one-way pointer node sequence).

slides.question: Q25: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a linear collection of nodes where each node contains pointers to both the next and the previous nodes.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Doubly Linked List** is defined as: is a linear collection of nodes where each node contains pointers to both the next and the previous nodes. (two-way pointer node sequence).

slides.question: Q26: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a stable, divide-and-conquer sorting algorithm that splits arrays in halves, sorts them, and merges them back.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Merge Sort** is defined as: is a stable, divide-and-conquer sorting algorithm that splits arrays in halves, sorts them, and merges them back. (O(n log n) stable comparison sort).

slides.question: Q27: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is an efficient search algorithm that repeatedly divides a sorted search interval in half to locate a target.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Binary Search** is defined as: is an efficient search algorithm that repeatedly divides a sorted search interval in half to locate a target. (O(log n) sorted array search).

slides.question: Q28: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a stack memory structure that stores information about active subroutines and functions in a running program.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Call Stack** is defined as: is a stack memory structure that stores information about active subroutines and functions in a running program. (active subroutine memory tracker).

slides.question: Q29: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is the programming operation used to retrieve or modify the value stored at the memory address held by a pointer.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Pointer dereferencing** is defined as: is the programming operation used to retrieve or modify the value stored at the memory address held by a pointer. (retrieving value from pointer address).

slides.question: Q30: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is an object-oriented programming concept allowing objects of different classes to be treated as objects of a common superclass.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Polymorphism** is defined as: is an object-oriented programming concept allowing objects of different classes to be treated as objects of a common superclass. (single interface multi-implementation capability).

slides.question: Q31: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a virtual table based on the result-set of an active SQL statement, containing no physical data of its own.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Database View** is defined as: is a virtual table based on the result-set of an active SQL statement, containing no physical data of its own. (virtual table query abstraction).

slides.question: Q32: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'provides a mechanism for storage and retrieval of data modeled in formats other than tabular relations (e.g. document, key-value).'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **NoSQL Database** is defined as: provides a mechanism for storage and retrieval of data modeled in formats other than tabular relations (e.g. document, key-value). (non-relational schema-less storage).

slides.question: Q33: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'represents the logical structure of a database using Entities, Attributes, and Relationships visually.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **ER Modeling** is defined as: represents the logical structure of a database using Entities, Attributes, and Relationships visually. (logical database structure visualization).

slides.question: Q34: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'reverts all changes made during the current transaction if a failure occurs before completion, ensuring atomicity.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Transaction Rollback** is defined as: reverts all changes made during the current transaction if a failure occurs before completion, ensuring atomicity. (reverting uncommitted transactional modifications).

slides.question: Q35: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'occurs when two concurrent transactions read and update the same data item, where one overwrite is lost.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Lost Update Anomaly** is defined as: occurs when two concurrent transactions read and update the same data item, where one overwrite is lost. (concurrent write overwrite anomaly).

slides.question: Q36: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'occurs when a transaction reads data that has been modified by another concurrent transaction but not yet committed.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Dirty Read** is defined as: occurs when a transaction reads data that has been modified by another concurrent transaction but not yet committed. (reading uncommitted transaction modifications).

slides.question: Q37: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'occurs when a transaction re-reads data it previously read and finds that another committed transaction has modified it.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Non-Repeatable Read** is defined as: occurs when a transaction re-reads data it previously read and finds that another committed transaction has modified it. (reading committed concurrent modifications).

slides.question: Q38: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'occurs when a transaction re-runs a query returning a set of rows and finds new rows added by another committed transaction.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Phantom Read** is defined as: occurs when a transaction re-runs a query returning a set of rows and finds new rows added by another committed transaction. (reading committed concurrent insertions).

slides.question: Q39: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a DBMS component that analyzes SQL statements and determines the most efficient execution plan based on statistics.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Query Optimizer** is defined as: is a DBMS component that analyzes SQL statements and determines the most efficient execution plan based on statistics. (efficient execution plan selector).

slides.question: Q40: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a procedural block of code that automatically executes in response to certain events on a particular table.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Database Trigger** is defined as: is a procedural block of code that automatically executes in response to certain events on a particular table. (automatic event-driven code block).

slides.question: Q41: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'promotes continuous iteration of development and testing throughout the software development life cycle.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Agile Methodology** is defined as: promotes continuous iteration of development and testing throughout the software development life cycle. (flexible iterative development paradigm).

slides.question: Q42: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a reusable, template-like solution to a commonly occurring design problem in software architecture.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Design Pattern** is defined as: is a reusable, template-like solution to a commonly occurring design problem in software architecture. (reusable architectural solution blueprint).

slides.question: Q43: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a software testing method by which individual source code components are tested to determine if they are fit for use.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Unit Testing** is defined as: is a software testing method by which individual source code components are tested to determine if they are fit for use. (isolated component validation).

slides.question: Q44: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a distributed version control system designed to track changes in source code during software development.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Git Version Control** is defined as: is a distributed version control system designed to track changes in source code during software development. (distributed source control management).

slides.question: Q45: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'automates software delivery phases: building, testing, and deploying changes to production environments.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **CI/CD Pipeline** is defined as: automates software delivery phases: building, testing, and deploying changes to production environments. (automated deployment automation).

slides.question: Q46: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a method of software testing that examines the functionality of an application without peering into its internal structures.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Black-box Testing** is defined as: is a method of software testing that examines the functionality of an application without peering into its internal structures. (functional specification validation).

slides.question: Q47: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a method of software testing that tests internal structures or workings of an application, requiring code visibility.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **White-box Testing** is defined as: is a method of software testing that tests internal structures or workings of an application, requiring code visibility. (internal code path validation).

slides.question: Q48: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'refers to the high-level structures of a software system, the discipline of creating such structures.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Software Architecture** is defined as: refers to the high-level structures of a software system, the discipline of creating such structures. (high-level structural system organization).

slides.question: Q49: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is an agile framework for managing complex work, utilizing sprints, daily standups, and product backlogs.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Scrum Framework** is defined as: is an agile framework for managing complex work, utilizing sprints, daily standups, and product backlogs. (sprint-based team collaboration).

slides.question: Q50: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'measures the strength of relationship between pieces of functionality within a single software module.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Cohesion** is defined as: measures the strength of relationship between pieces of functionality within a single software module. (single module functional focus score).

slides.title: Discussion & Review 💬
slides.content: Review your answers for Mock Exam 2. Pay special attention to topics where you selected incorrect answers!

box.content: You have completed Computer Science Mock Exam 2.
box.note: Solve all 10 mock exams to master the 500 Computer Science questions!
